knowledge_items.title: Eye Tracking Continuous Performance Test for ADHD Assessment: A Randomized Comparison
key_findings: Eye-tracking integrated continuous performance test (CPT) shows moderate discriminability for ADHD (AUC=0.74)
Omission errors and reaction time variability are the strongest CPT-based predictors of ADHD diagnosis
Combining eye-tracking metrics with CPT scores improves classification by 6% over CPT alone
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Randomized comparison of standard CPT vs eye-tracking integrated CPT in 312 children aged 7–13 (156 ADHD, 156 controls)
limitations: Short-term assessment may not capture trait stability

knowledge_items.title: Neuroimaging Predictors of Post-Stroke Cognitive Recovery: A Longitudinal MRI Study
key_findings: Hippocampal volume at 3 months post-stroke predicts cognitive recovery trajectory at 12 months (β=0.42, p<0.01)
White matter integrity in the fornix is associated with memory recovery independent of lesion volume
Early neuroimaging biomarkers account for 34% of variance in 12-month cognitive outcomes
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Longitudinal cohort study with 3-month and 12-month MRI follow-up, n=198 ischemic stroke survivors, cognitive battery at both timepoints
limitations: Moderate sample size for neuroimaging analyses
Single neuroimaging modality without PET or DTI integration

knowledge_items.title: Pharmacogenetic Predictors of SSRI Response in Major Depressive Disorder: A Genome-Wide Association Study
key_findings: A polygenic risk score comprising 47 SNPs predicts SSRI non-response at 8 weeks (OR=1.68, p=3.2×10⁻⁸)
CYP2C19 metabolizer status significantly affects escitalopram plasma levels and side effect burden
Pharmacogenetic-guided treatment selection reduces time to remission by 2.3 weeks compared to standard care
knowledge_items.methods_detail: GWAS of SSRI treatment response in 2,847 MDD patients with prospective 8-week treatment outcome assessment
limitations: European ancestry only limits cross-ethnic applicability
GWAS signal concentrated in non-coding regions with unclear biological mechanism
Single antidepressant class (SSRIs) may not generalize to other treatments
